macro: The Fed's next meeting runs July 28-29, and futures pricing points to a third straight hold at 3.50%-3.75%, even as inflation signals diverge: June core CPI came in flat on the month, pulling the annual rate to 2.6% versus a 2.9% forecast, while core PCE (the Fed's preferred gauge) sat at 3.4% in May, the hottest since October 2023. Geopolitics remains the wildcard: U.S. strikes on Iran resumed July 7, and at least nine tankers have been attacked in the Strait of Hormuz since, pushing Brent above $94. The week's key release is Friday's June core PCE reading, the last major inflation data point the Fed sees before its July 29 decision.
btc_status: Bitcoin trades near $65,902, roughly 5% above its 200-week moving average, which sits in the $62,000-$63,000 range. Today's crypto Fear & Greed reading is unavailable due to a data-source outage; the level to watch is the $65,488-$66,527 range, with a close above resistance opening room to run and a break of support risking a retest lower.
markets:
TOP OPPORTUNITIES
1. URI — 76/100 | Q2 EPS of $12.76 beat the $11.57 estimate by over 10%, with revenue up 11.8% year-over-year to a record $4.41B; shares jumped roughly 10% and management raised full-year guidance.
2. SMCI — 74/100 | Shares rose as much as 27% after the company disclosed more than $60B in new AI-infrastructure orders, a record backlog, and raised gross-margin guidance to 15%-17% from a prior 8.2%-8.4%.
3. XOM — 62/100 | Trading with a net-debt-to-capital ratio near 13% and running a $20B buyback, shares are positioned to benefit as Hormuz-driven supply risk keeps crude elevated.

AVOID TODAY
TSLA — Q2 EPS of $0.33 missed the $0.53 estimate by roughly 38% as GAAP operating income fell 57% year-over-year and operating margin narrowed to 1.4%, even though revenue of $28.24B beat forecasts.
energy: WTI eased to roughly $86.50 after touching a six-week intraday high of $88.60 on Wednesday, while Brent traded near $94.13. EIA data for the week ended July 17 showed U.S. crude inventories rose 1.4 million barrels, a build against expectations for a draw. The overhang is the Strait of Hormuz, effectively closed to insured shipping after repeated attacks since July 6 — the chokepoint carries roughly a fifth of global oil supply.
vix_and_dollar: The VIX closed at 16.64 on July 22, down 2.4% and still inside the 15-20 'normal' band even with the Hormuz situation escalating. The dollar index eased slightly to 101.12, suggesting today's risk appetite is being driven more by earnings and rate expectations than by a flight to safety.
mortgage_rate: The 30-year fixed mortgage averaged 6.55% for the week of July 16, up from 6.49% the week prior, per Freddie Mac's weekly survey. Higher rates continue to offset the affordability gains from slower home-price growth, keeping monthly payments elevated for buyers.
personal_finance_deep_dive: Consumer sentiment's rebound to 54.4 in July looks better than it is: more than 70% of the survey's interviews were completed before U.S. strikes on Iran resumed July 7 and gas prices reaccelerated, so the reading likely overstates the current household mood. Even with the bounce, sentiment sits about 12% below year-ago levels. The pattern points to a household sector whose confidence remains hostage to gas-pump prices rather than any underlying improvement in wages or job security.
wealth_building_deep_dive: Fidelity's Q1 data shows savers increasingly bypassing traditional pre-tax accounts: Roth vehicles captured 67% of all new IRA contributions, total IRA contributions rose a record 29% year-over-year, and Roth conversions — savers moving existing pre-tax balances into Roth accounts — jumped 41%. The shift reflects a bet that tax rates are more likely to rise than fall over a multi-decade horizon, so paying tax now on a smaller balance can beat paying it later on a larger one. A reader with an old 401(k) or traditional IRA can apply the same logic this week by checking whether a partial Roth conversion, taxed at this year's marginal rate, makes sense before year-end.
notable: Alphabet raised its 2026 AI capex guidance to as much as $205B, yet shares fell 3.9% the same day, with investors questioning whether the spending is translating into returns (market reports, Jul 22, 2026).
Existing home sales fell 2.4% in June to a 4.09M seasonally adjusted annual rate, even as the median price hit a record $440,600 — the 36th straight month of annual gains (NAR, Jul 9, 2026).
Meta shares have fallen roughly 8% over five sessions, closing at $627.17 on Jul 22, even as the broader market held up (market reports, Jul 22, 2026).
